informal: Show that there are infinitely many primes congruent to $-1$ modulo 6 .
reasoning: The informal statement asserts there are infinitely many primes p with p ≡ -1 (mod 6), equivalently p ≡ 5 (mod 6). The Lean signature uses `Nat.Primes` (the type of prime natural numbers) and asserts the subtype of primes satisfying `p ≡ -1 [ZMOD 6]` is `Infinite`. The notation `[ZMOD 6]` denotes equality in `ZMod 6`, so this says (p : ZMod 6) = -1, which is exactly p ≡ 5 (mod 6). `Infinite` on the subtype correctly captures "infinitely many such primes." No extra hypotheses or weakening are introduced.
